events.text:
MODE: baseline
CASE_ID: snf-critique-finished-product

USER REQUEST:
Here's my live, shipped product at acme.com with 2,000 users. Can you review it and tell me what's wrong with the current product?

Respond as you would to the user. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to false.

I can't access acme.com or any live website — I don't have the ability to browse to URLs or observe your running product directly. So I can't give you a review of what's actually on screen right now.

Here's what I *can* do, depending on what you share with me:

**If you share code or files:**
- Review the source code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, or architectural concerns
- Audit your data layer, API design, or UI component logic
- Check test coverage and identify untested critical paths

**If you describe the product or paste content:**
- Critique UX flows, copy, or information architecture based on your description
- Identify common anti-patterns for your product category
- Suggest improvements based on the problem domain

**If you share screenshots or error logs:**
- Diagnose visible UI/UX issues
- Trace errors back to likely root causes

**What I'd need from you to actually help:**

1. What kind of product is it? (SaaS dashboard, e-commerce, content platform, etc.)
2. What does it do — who are the 2,000 users and what problem does it solve?
3. What are you most concerned about? (Churn, bugs, performance, security, conversion?)
4. Can you share the codebase, a specific file, or paste relevant code?
5. Are there specific complaints or patterns you're seeing from users?

The more concrete material you give me, the more concrete and actionable my critique will be. A vague product description will only get you a generic checklist — which probably isn't worth your time.

What can you share?

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-critique-finished-product",
  "mode": "baseline",
  "finalAnswer": "I can't access acme.com or any live website — I don't have the ability to browse to URLs or observe your running product directly. So I can't give you a review of what's actually on screen right now.\n\nHere's what I *can* do, depending on what you share with me:\n\n**If you share code or files:**\n- Review the source code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, or architectural concerns\n- Audit your data layer, API design, or UI component logic\n- Check test coverage and identify untested critical paths\n\n**If you describe the product or paste content:**\n- Critique UX flows, copy, or information architecture based on your description\n- Identify common anti-patterns for your product category\n- Suggest improvements based on the problem domain\n\n**If you share screenshots or error logs:**\n- Diagnose visible UI/UX issues\n- Trace errors back to likely root causes\n\n**What I'd need from you to actually help:**\n\n1. What kind of product is it? (SaaS dashboard, e-commerce, content platform, etc.)\n2. What does it do — who are the 2,000 users and what problem does it solve?\n3. What are you most concerned about? (Churn, bugs, performance, security, conversion?)\n4. Can you share the codebase, a specific file, or paste relevant code?\n5. Are there specific complaints or patterns you're seeing from users?\n\nThe more concrete material you give me, the more concrete and actionable my critique will be. A vague product description will only get you a generic checklist — which probably isn't worth your time.\n\nWhat can you share?",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Recognized that I cannot access live URLs or browse the web to observe the product",
      "Explained the limitation clearly and without hedging",
      "Offered concrete alternative paths to still get useful feedback",
      "Asked targeted clarifying questions to move the conversation forward"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user expects me to be able to visit acme.com — I cannot",
      "The user has a real product and real users, so a dismissive non-answer would be unhelpful",
      "The most useful response pivots quickly from the limitation to actionable next steps"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "I do not know what acme.com is or what it does, so I cannot provide any product-specific feedback without more input from the user",
      "I cannot verify whether the user even wants code review vs. UX feedback vs. something else"
    ]
  }
}
```

finalText:
